channel.title: Christie's on Shipwreck, the Rescue -- JMW Turner
channel.description: Recent content in Christie's on Shipwreck, the Rescue -- JMW Turner

item.title: Chapter 17: Back to Christie's
item.description:
Chapter 17

Back to Christie’s

Excerpt from Art World’s Dirty Little Secret by R. Malcolm Setters / Graham Setters

Back to Christie’s

After several months without contact with Christie’s, Bob finally decided to phone Jeremy Rex-Parkes to determine why his seemingly smooth correspondence had been cut off so abruptly. Jeremy’s cheerful greeting betrayed his own deception. As it turned out he had been forwarding Bob’s e-mails to Mr. Nicholas White of the picture department expecting him to respond to Bob, but for some unknown reason they went no further. According to Jeremy, Mr. White said he answered the inquiries; but the truth of the matter was Bob heard nothing from anyone. After a brooding wait, he could only assume he’d been brushed off.

item.title: Chapter 18: Muddled Intentions
item.description:
Chapter 18

Muddled Intentions

Excerpt from Art World’s Dirty Little Secret by R. Malcolm Setters / Graham Setters

Muddled Intentions

Government regulation of historic artwork is one peripheral issue that concerns art experts and affects the value of artwork. It is easier to move, sell, export, and bequeath decorative art—that which does not fall into the restrictive category of national treasure. Once catalogued and traceable it becomes risky to do anything with it across borders without proper documentation. And by now I had slipped out of without being duly recognized. Who had been responsible for this faux pas?
